genre_refs: The Patriot (2000 film, Mel Gibson); American Revolution / colonial militia imagery
description: A designed movie-still meme posted by the U.S. Department of Labor. The image is a warm, sepia/teal color-graded film still from the 2000 movie 'The Patriot' showing Mel Gibson's character (Benjamin Martin) kneeling in a hay field during battle, gripping the staff of a tattered early American flag (the 13-star Betsy Ross / Grand Union style flag with red-and-white stripes and a circle of white stars) which billows to the right. Smoke and a blurred battlefield fill the background. Across the bottom in large distressed/serif white capital letters with subtle shadow is the slogan 'HOLD THE LINE.' The technique appropriates a Revolutionary War cinematic image of patriotic defiance to dramatize the Department's 'America First' labor agenda. The tweet caption reads: 'We’ll never stop fighting for the American Way of Life. AMERICA FIRST. AMERICANS FIRST.' Target audience is patriotic/nationalist supporters; the messaging frames domestic labor policy as a heroic last stand.
notable_text: HOLD THE LINE. / (caption) We’ll never stop fighting for the American Way of Life. AMERICA FIRST. AMERICANS FIRST.
